initial_request_score.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: Impact of subnasal lip lift on lip aesthetic: a systematic review
A systematic search was carried out using the PubMed, Science Direct, Scopus, Cochrane Library and EMBASE databases, based on records published until July 2020.
assess whether the subnasal lip lift technique improves lip aesthetics and maintains its stability in adult patients

issues.claim: stability duration ranging from 12 to 59.1 months
issues.reason: This range is reported as a result finding in the Abstract and Results, not as a pre-defined goal or inclusion criterion in the Methods.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: Change quantifiable_goal to reflect the intent to assess stability duration without specifying the result range, e.g., 'Assess stability duration and lip aesthetic improvement metrics'.

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: totalling 464 articles. 33 articles in PubMed, 42 in Scopus, 158 in Science Direct, no articles in Cochrane and 231 articles in EMBASE

issues.reason: No citations provided for the database search methodology despite text evidence being available.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Add reference to the Abstract or Methods section describing the search strategy.

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: only 4 studies met the inclusion criteria, while 44 were excluded

issues.reason: No citations provided for the screening process despite text evidence being available.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Add reference to the Methods section describing Literature selection.

evidence.quote: The quality of the included studies was assessed using the Cochrane Handbook for Systematic Reviews of Interventions.
Higgins J, Thomas J, editors. Cochrane handbook for systematic reviews of interventions [Internet]. Version 6,2019
92.4% of subnasal lip lift cases were performed in women with an age range between 21 and 65 years (mean of 36.6 years).
maintaining results for varying periods of time (from 12 to 59.1 months).

The included studies suggest that the subnasal lip lift improves lip aesthetics and maintains its stability for a certain period of time ( ±28 months) in adult patients. However, as there is still no standardization...
